labels.label:
ExecuteSQL is a trigger-based processor that runs repeatedly based on its scheduling configuration. It doesn't automatically know when to stop.

The Default Behavior:
text
Schedule: Timer-driven, Run Schedule: 0 sec
Result: 
  - Returns ALL results in a single FlowFile
  - Output format: Avro (by default)
  - ExecuteSQL runs as fast as possible
  - Every run executes the SAME query
  - Creates a new FlowFile with results each time
  - INFINITE LOOP of the same query!
Same as QueryDatabaseTable but with Record API support for flexible output formats

ExecuteSQLRecord - The Modern Alternative
This is the newer, record-aware version of ExecuteSQL.

Key difference:
Instead of forcing Avro output, ExecuteSQLRecord uses NiFi's Record API, allowing flexible output formats (JSON, CSV, Avro, etc.).

QueryDatabaseTable - The Incremental Ingestion Processor
This processor is designed for batch ETL where you need to periodically fetch only NEW or CHANGED records.

How it tracks state:

Stores the last processed value of a "maximum-value column"

Common columns: id (incrementing) or last_updated (timestamp)

Output: Avro format (similar to ExecuteSQL)

⚠️ Important limitations:

Only supports single table queries (no JOINs)

Cannot handle complex WHERE clauses

Works best with simple, incrementing columns

PutDatabaseRecord - High-Performance Record Writer
This is the recommended processor for writing data to databases.

Why it's better than PutSQL:

Handles batch operations efficiently

Automatic type mapping

No need to write SQL manually

Much higher throughput
